name: Belgorod Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the chalk hills of Belgorod (whose name means 'white town') and purity
the fertile fields and forests of the Central Black Earth Region
the courage of the people and blood shed in defence of the homeland
the rich black earth (chernozem) of the oblast

description: A flag divided into four quarters — white at upper hoist, green at upper fly, red at lower hoist, and black at lower fly — with the oblast's coat of arms (a black eagle and a golden lion) charged on the white upper-hoist quarter.
history: Adopted on 22 June 2000 by the Belgorod Oblast Duma. The four-colour cruciform design references the historic regimental colours of the Belgorod Infantry Regiment of the Imperial Russian Army.

features.symbolism: the four colours represent the historic banners that fought on Belgorod soil: white for the chalk hills, green for the agricultural fields, red for the courage and blood shed in defence of the homeland, and black for the fertile black earth.
the white upper-hoist quarter bears the historic emblem of Belgorod.
a black eagle and a golden lion, drawn from the 1730 imperial coat of arms of the Belgorod Infantry Regiment, recalling the victory at the Battle of Poltava (1709).
